notes: Twenty-second validated clue batch compiled from official school websites.
Clues intentionally avoid revealing enrollment size, grade span, or directional/proximity information because those are gameplay feedback channels.

clues.text: Official materials say the school's mission is to transform lives.
Its board documents say the school is transforming lives and enriching communities by delivering high-quality educational choice to families.
The school says it creates a warm, welcoming environment where families feel valued and supported and students are empowered to reach their full potential.
Official materials say the school's board is made up of local civic and community leaders who volunteer their time and energy.
Official pages list Dr. Michael Fuga as principal.
Official pages list Gerald McNair as board president.
Its mission says the school provides quality instruction, high expectations, and a standards-based curriculum with a focus on STEAM.
Official materials say the school opened in 2021.
The school says it is a replication of the nationally acclaimed Doral Academy of Technology K-8 School in Miami.
Official materials say the school earned and maintained a B rating and received the NC Academic Growth Award for three consecutive years.
Official administration pages list Annaliet Echaniz as founding principal.
Official board pages list David Correa as board chair.
Its mission says the school promotes high academic achievement by increasing student engagement through a workshop model that encourages students to take ownership of their learning.
Official materials say the school uses an immersive and interdisciplinary STEM approach.
The school highlights partnerships with Discovery Place Education Studio and Teachers College of Columbia's Reading and Writing Project.
Official materials say students can choose from the Leadership Academy, Innovation Academy, and Business Academy.
Official administration pages list Deanna Smith as executive director.
Official materials refer to students as Titans.
Its homepage tagline is "Finish School. Accelerate Your Future."
Official materials say the school is built around accelerated credit earning with a non-traditional approach.
The school says students begin by creating an Individualized Success Plan, or ISP.
Official schedule pages show three separate 4-hour sessions each day.
Official pages list Tamara Wynn as principal and Dr. Jonathan Kay as regional director.
Official governance pages list Morgan Kelly Tyler as board president.
Its mission says the school serves students who have dropped out or are likely to drop out by providing a non-traditional learning environment leading to a diploma and postsecondary success.
Official materials say the school is built around three pillars: Flexible, Personalized, and Supportive.
The school says every student starts with an Individualized Success Plan and a personalized Success Dashboard.
Official materials say the program includes career coaches, wraparound services, and multilingual course translation support.
Official pages identify Tom Hanley as executive principal.
Official governance pages list Michael Byers as board president.
